attributes.action: Prevent double taxation on PHEV drivers under road pricing
attributes.additional_details:
On a 300-mile journey in a PHEV with a 40-mile electric range, around 260 miles are powered by petrol. These petrol miles include fuel duty and VAT within the cost of fuel. A PHEV would pay £17.00 inc 1.5p and fuel duty at current rates

A fully electric vehicle travelling the same 300 miles would pay £9.00 road charge, as no fuel duty applies.

This creates a clear imbalance, where PHEV drivers pay more for the same road use.
attributes.background:
We call on the Government to ensure that any per-mile road pricing system does not result in double taxation for plug-in hybrid (PHEV) drivers. 
PHEVs were promoted as a lower-emission technology. Applying a flat per-mile charge alongside existing fuel duty risks unfairly penalising these drivers.

rejection.details:
We already have a petition open on this subject which you may wish to sign:

https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/755668
